meta.title: Historical Events
meta.subtitle: Turning points across world history — searchable.

glossary.heading: Periods — how the historical eras are bounded

terms.Ancient (to 500 CE): From the earliest records through the fall of the western Roman empire.
terms.Medieval (500–1500): The middle ages across Europe, the Islamic world, and Asia.
terms.Early modern (1500–1800): The age of exploration, reformation, and revolutions.
terms.20th century onward: World wars, decolonisation, and the digital age.

meta.footer: A placeholder topic seeded with sample events. Dates are scholarly estimates where exact dates are unknown.

entries.name: Founding of Rome
entries.desc: Traditional date for the founding of the city of Rome, later the centre of the Roman Republic and Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Marathon
entries.desc: Athenian-led victory over the first Persian invasion of Greece, a defining moment for the Greek city-states.

entries.name: Unification of China under Qin
entries.desc: Qin Shi Huang united the warring states into the first centralised Chinese empire.

entries.name: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
entries.desc: Deposition of Romulus Augustulus, conventionally marking the end of the western Roman Empire.

entries.name: The Hijra
entries.desc: Muhammad's migration from Mecca to Medina, the starting point of the Islamic calendar.

entries.name: Norman Conquest of England
entries.desc: William of Normandy's victory at Hastings reshaped English language, law, and aristocracy.

entries.name: Fall of Constantinople
entries.desc: Ottoman capture of the Byzantine capital, often taken to mark the end of the Middle Ages.

entries.name: Columbus Reaches the Americas
entries.desc: First of Columbus's voyages, opening sustained European contact with the Americas.

entries.name: Arrival of the First Fleet
entries.desc: British ships established the first European colony in Australia at Sydney Cove.

entries.name: French Revolution Begins
entries.desc: Storming of the Bastille opened a decade of upheaval that toppled the French monarchy.

entries.name: Meiji Restoration
entries.desc: Restoration of imperial rule in Japan and the start of rapid modernisation.

entries.name: Berlin Conference
entries.desc: European powers formalised the partition and colonisation of Africa.

entries.name: Partition of India
entries.desc: Independence and division of British India into India and Pakistan.

entries.name: Apollo 11 Moon Landing
entries.desc: First crewed landing on the Moon, by the United States' Apollo programme.

entries.name: Siege of Van
entries.desc: Defense of Van (1915), the Armenian population's self-defense against Ottoman forces during the Armenian genocide.

entries.name: Battle of Fahl
entries.desc: Major battle in the Muslim conquest of Byzantine Syria, fought by Rashidun Caliphate forces near Pella (Fahl).

entries.name: Polish–Muscovite War
entries.desc: The Polish–Muscovite War (1605–18), a conflict between the Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Tsardom of Russia.

entries.name: Treaty of Hünkâr İskelesi
entries.desc: Treaty between the Russian Empire and the Ottoman Empire establishing a mutual defence alliance.

entries.name: Battle of Madagascar
entries.desc: Allied campaign to capture the Vichy French-controlled island of Madagascar during World War II.

entries.name: Battle of Guandu
entries.desc: Cao Cao's decisive victory over Yuan Shao's larger forces, a turning point of the late Eastern Han dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Puebla
entries.desc: Mexican victory over French forces at Puebla during the second French intervention in Mexico.

entries.name: Battle of Auray
entries.desc: Decisive confrontation of the Breton War of Succession, part of the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Pig War
entries.desc: Largely bloodless 1859 border confrontation between the United States and the British Colony of Vancouver Island.

entries.name: Siege of Vicksburg
entries.desc: Final major military action of the Vicksburg campaign, ending in a decisive Union victory in the American Civil War.

entries.name: Battle of Los Angeles
entries.desc: A rumored 1942 Japanese air attack on Los Angeles triggered an anti-aircraft artillery barrage over the city.

entries.name: Battle of Rivoli
entries.desc: Napoleon's decisive defeat of Austrian forces near Rivoli, the climax of the Italian campaign of 1796–97.

entries.name: King William's War
entries.desc: North American theater of the Nine Years' War, fought between New France and New England and their Native allies.

entries.name: Battle of Smolensk
entries.desc: Battle of Smolensk (1812), fought during Napoleon's invasion of Russia.

entries.name: Battle of Vaslui
entries.desc: Stephen III of Moldavia's victory over Ottoman forces near Vaslui in 1475.

entries.name: Battle of Maipú
entries.desc: Decisive Patriot victory over Spanish royalists near Santiago, completing Chile's independence.

entries.name: Battle of Yamen
entries.desc: Naval battle marking the last stand of the Song dynasty against the invading Yuan dynasty forces.

entries.name: Battle of Neerwinden
entries.desc: Battle of Neerwinden (1793), a French defeat to Austrian forces during the War of the First Coalition.

entries.name: Siege of Bursa
entries.desc: Long Ottoman siege that ended in the capture of Bursa (Prusa) in 1326, an early Ottoman conquest.

entries.name: Battle of Fulford
entries.desc: Harald Hardrada and Tostig Godwinson's defeat of the Northern Earls near York, days before Stamford Bridge.

entries.name: Battle of Worcester
entries.desc: Oliver Cromwell's Parliamentarian army defeated Charles II's Royalist forces at Worcester, ending the Wars of the Three Kingdoms.

entries.name: Union of Krewo
entries.desc: Grand Duke Jogaila of Lithuania pledged to marry Queen Jadwiga of Poland, laying the groundwork for a Polish-Lithuanian union.

entries.name: Battle of Alcácer Quibir
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under King Sebastian were destroyed by a Moroccan army near Ksar-el-Kebir, ending Portugal's independent monarchy.

entries.name: Battle of Rorke's Drift
entries.desc: A small British garrison defended a mission station against a much larger Zulu force during the Anglo-Zulu War.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Graupius
entries.desc: Roman forces under Agricola defeated a Caledonian army in what is now Scotland, per Tacitus's account.

entries.name: Battle of Ad Decimum
entries.desc: Byzantine general Belisarius defeated the Vandal army near Carthage, opening the reconquest of North Africa.

entries.name: Great Molasses Flood
entries.desc: A ruptured storage tank released a wave of molasses through Boston's North End, killing 21 people.

entries.name: Battle of Princeton
entries.desc: George Washington's forces defeated a British rearguard at Princeton, New Jersey, boosting American morale after Trenton.

entries.name: Battle of the Camel
entries.desc: Caliph Ali defeated a rebel coalition led by Aisha, Talha, and Zubayr outside Basra, in the first civil war of Islam.

entries.name: Vandalic War
entries.desc: Byzantine forces under Justinian I reconquered North Africa from the Vandal Kingdom.

entries.name: Battle of Omdurman
entries.desc: Anglo-Egyptian forces under Kitchener crushed the Mahdist army near Omdurman, Sudan, cementing British control of the region.

entries.name: Battle of Mons Lactarius
entries.desc: Byzantine forces defeated the Ostrogoths near Mount Vesuvius, ending organized Gothic resistance in Italy.

entries.name: Battle of Annual
entries.desc: Riffian Berber forces inflicted a catastrophic defeat on the Spanish Army in northeastern Morocco, triggering political crisis in Spain.

entries.name: Smolensk War
entries.desc: The Polish–Lithuanian Commonwealth fought Muscovy over control of Smolensk.

entries.name: Battle of Formigny
entries.desc: A decisive French victory over England near Formigny hastened the end of the Hundred Years' War in Normandy.

entries.name: Battle of Wake Island
entries.desc: Japanese forces captured the American-held Pacific atoll of Wake Island after a fierce two-week defense.

entries.name: Battles of Kawanakajima
entries.desc: Takeda Shingen and Uesugi Kenshin fought a series of inconclusive battles for control of the Kawanakajima plain in Sengoku-era Japan.

entries.name: Battle of Boyacá
entries.desc: The Battle of Boyacá (1819), also known as the Battle of Boyacá Bridge was a decisive victory by a combined army of Venezuelan and New Granadan troops along with a British Legion led by General Simon Bolivar over the III Division of the Spanish Expeditionary Army of Costa Firme commanded by Spanish Colonel José Barreiro.

entries.name: First Anglo-Dutch War
entries.desc: The First Anglo-Dutch War, or First Dutch War, was a naval conflict between the Commonwealth of England and the Dutch Republic, largely caused by disputes over trade.

entries.name: Second Ottoman–Venetian War
entries.desc: The Second Ottoman–Venetian War was fought from 1499 to 1503 between the Ottoman Empire and the Republic of Venice for control of contested lands in the Aegean, Ionian, and Adriatic seas.

entries.name: First Ottoman–Venetian War
entries.desc: The First Ottoman–Venetian War was fought between the Republic of Venice with its allies and the Ottoman Empire from 1463 to 1479, resulting in the loss of several Venetian holdings in Albania and Greece.

entries.name: Battle of Edessa
entries.desc: The Battle of Edessa took place between the armies of the Roman Empire under Valerian and the Sasanian Empire under Shapur I in 260, resulting in the capture of a Roman emperor for the first time.

entries.name: War of the Oranges
entries.desc: The War of the Oranges was a brief 1801 conflict in which Spanish forces, instigated and supported by France, invaded Portugal, resulting in the Treaty of Badajoz.

entries.name: Webster–Ashburton Treaty
entries.desc: The Webster–Ashburton Treaty resolved several border issues between the United States and the British North American colonies, signed in Washington, DC in 1842.

entries.name: Musket Wars
entries.desc: The Musket Wars were a series of as many as 3,000 battles and raids fought throughout New Zealand among Māori between 1806 and 1845, after Māori first obtained muskets.

entries.name: Battle of Didgori
entries.desc: The Battle of Didgori was fought between the armies of the Kingdom of Georgia and the Seljuk Empire near Tbilisi in 1121, resulting in a devastating defeat for the Seljuk-led coalition.

entries.name: Battle of Toba-Fushimi
entries.desc: The Battle of Toba–Fushimi occurred between pro-Imperial and Tokugawa shogunate forces during the Boshin War in Japan, beginning on 27 January 1868 near Kyoto.

entries.name: Battle of Orsha
entries.desc: The Battle of Orsha was fought on 8 September 1514 between the allied forces of the Grand Duchy of Lithuania and the Crown of the Kingdom of Poland and the army of the Grand Duchy of Moscow.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Burmese War
entries.desc: The Third Anglo-Burmese War was the final of three 19th-century wars between the Burmese and the British, ending in the loss of Burmese sovereignty under the Konbaung dynasty.

entries.name: Continuation War
entries.desc: The Continuation War, also known as the Second Soviet–Finnish War, was fought by Finland and Nazi Germany against the Soviet Union from 1941 to 1944, ending with the Moscow Armistice.

entries.name: Sixth Russo-Turkish War
entries.desc: The Russo-Turkish War of 1768–1774 was a major armed conflict that saw Russian armies victorious against the Ottoman Empire, bringing Crimea into the Russian sphere of influence.

entries.name: Seventh Russo-Turkish War
entries.desc: The Russo-Turkish War of 1787–1792 involved an unsuccessful attempt by the Ottoman Empire to regain lands lost to Russia in the previous Russo-Turkish War, fought alongside several concurrent European wars.

entries.name: Battle of Roncevaux Pass
entries.desc: The Battle of Roncevaux Pass in 778 saw a large force of Basques ambush part of Charlemagne's army in the Pyrenees, on the present border between France and Spain.

entries.name: Battle of Loos
entries.desc: The biggest British attack of 1915, fought on the Western Front in France and marking the war's first British use of poison gas.

entries.name: Hundred Years' War
entries.desc: A prolonged conflict between the kingdoms of England and France over rival claims to the French throne, fought intermittently from 1337 to 1453.

entries.name: Spanish–American War
entries.desc: A brief 1898 war between Spain and the United States, sparked by the sinking of the USS Maine, that ended Spanish colonial rule in Cuba, Puerto Rico, Guam, and the Philippines.

entries.name: Third Battle of Panipat
entries.desc: The Maratha Empire suffered a devastating defeat to the invading Durrani Empire near Panipat in 1761, reshaping the balance of power in northern India.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Afghan War
entries.desc: A short 1919 war in which Afghanistan's new emir declared jihad against British India, ultimately securing full Afghan independence in foreign affairs.

entries.name: Battle of Chrysopolis
entries.desc: Constantine I's decisive victory over co-emperor Licinius near Chrysopolis in 324, leaving Constantine sole ruler of the Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Callinicum
entries.desc: A hard-fought 531 clash in which Belisarius's Byzantine army was checked by a Sasanian cavalry force under Azarethes.

entries.name: First Vienna Award
entries.desc: A 1938 arbitration following the Munich Agreement that forced Czechoslovakia to cede southern Slovakia and Carpathian Ruthenia to Hungary.

entries.name: Battle of Kolín
entries.desc: Austrian forces under Count von Daun defeated Frederick the Great's Prussian army near Kolín in 1757, the first major battle Frederick lost.

entries.name: Wars of the Roses
entries.desc: A series of dynastic civil wars fought between the houses of Lancaster and York for control of the English throne from 1455 to 1487.

entries.name: Battle of Neretva
entries.desc: A major 1943 Axis offensive against Yugoslav Partisan forces along the Neretva river, one of the largest confrontations of the Yugoslav theatre of World War II.

entries.name: Battle of the Ebro
entries.desc: The longest and largest battle of the Spanish Civil War, fought along the Ebro River in Catalonia between July and November 1938.

entries.name: Battle of Akroinon
entries.desc: Byzantine forces halted a major Umayyad invasion of Anatolia near Akroinon in 740, checking Arab expansion into Byzantine territory.

entries.name: Indian Mutiny of 1857
entries.desc: A widespread uprising of sepoys and civilians against East India Company rule, beginning at Meerut in 1857 and reshaping British governance of India.

entries.name: Battle of Flodden
entries.desc: An English army defeated an invading Scottish force under James IV near Flodden in 1513, part of the wider War of the League of Cambrai.

entries.name: Battle of Verdun
entries.desc: The longest battle of the First World War, fought from February to December 1916 as German forces attacked French defences north of Verdun.

entries.name: Battle of Kursk
entries.desc: The largest tank battle in history, fought in the summer of 1943 as Germany's last major offensive on the Eastern Front ended in Soviet victory.

entries.name: La Noche Triste
entries.desc: Hernán Cortés and his Spanish and native allied forces were driven out of the Aztec capital Tenochtitlan in a bloody 1520 retreat.

entries.name: Battle of the Somme
entries.desc: One of the largest and bloodiest battles of the First World War, fought by British and French forces against Germany along the Somme in 1916.

entries.name: Battle of Chickamauga
entries.desc: Confederate victory over Union forces in northwest Georgia, the most significant Union defeat in the war's Western Theater.

entries.name: Battle of Inab
entries.desc: Zengid forces under Nur al-Din Zengi destroyed the army of Prince Raymond of Antioch during the Second Crusade, killing Raymond.

entries.name: Battle of Ourique
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under Afonso Henriques defeated an Almoravid army, a battle later mythologized as founding the Kingdom of Portugal.

entries.name: Indonesian National Revolution
entries.desc: Armed and diplomatic struggle between the Republic of Indonesia and the Dutch Empire following Indonesia's 1945 declaration of independence.

entries.name: Battle of Fei River
entries.desc: Heavily outnumbered Eastern Jin forces decisively defeated the Former Qin dynasty on the Fei River, ending Fu Jian's bid to unify China.

entries.name: Treaty of Brétigny
entries.desc: Treaty between Edward III of England and John II of France that ended the first phase of the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Battle of Tewkesbury
entries.desc: Decisive Yorkist victory over Lancastrian forces in the Wars of the Roses, killing the Lancastrian heir Edward of Westminster.

entries.name: Battle of Evesham
entries.desc: Future King Edward I defeated and killed rebel baron Simon de Montfort, ending the Second Barons' War.

entries.name: Battle of Austerlitz
entries.desc: Napoleon's decisive victory over the combined Russian and Austrian armies, among the most significant engagements of the Napoleonic Wars.

entries.name: Battle of Lyndanisse
entries.desc: Danish forces defeated Estonian tribes during the Northern Crusades, allowing King Valdemar II to claim northern Estonia.

entries.name: Battle of Aljubarrota
entries.desc: Portuguese forces under John I decisively defeated Castile, securing Portuguese independence and John's claim to the throne.

entries.name: Third Anglo-Dutch War
entries.desc: Naval conflict between the Dutch Republic and an England allied with France, part of the wider Franco-Dutch War.

entries.name: Treaty of Verdun
entries.desc: Division of the Carolingian Empire among the three surviving sons of Louis the Pious, ending the Carolingian civil war.

entries.name: Ridda Wars
entries.desc: Campaigns launched by Caliph Abu Bakr against rebellious Arabian tribes following the death of Muhammad, consolidating the Rashidun Caliphate.

entries.name: 1920 Haiyuan earthquake
entries.desc: A catastrophic magnitude earthquake struck Haiyuan County in northwest China, one of the deadliest in history.

entries.name: 1908 Messina earthquake
entries.desc: A devastating earthquake and tsunami struck the Strait of Messina in southern Italy, killing around 120,000 people.

entries.name: 365 Crete earthquake
entries.desc: A massive undersea earthquake near Crete caused widespread destruction and tsunamis across the eastern Mediterranean.

entries.name: Winter War
entries.desc: The Soviet Union invaded Finland, which despite being outmatched inflicted heavy losses before the Moscow Peace Treaty ended the war.

entries.name: Warsaw Convention
entries.desc: International convention regulating airline liability for international carriage of passengers, baggage, and cargo.

entries.name: Polish–Russian War of 1792
entries.desc: War between the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth and the Russian Empire under Catherine the Great, allied with the Targowica Confederation.

entries.name: Battle of Muret
entries.desc: Simon de Montfort's small force of French crusaders routed a much larger army of Aragonese and Occitan lords, the decisive battle of the Albigensian Crusade.

entries.name: Italian War of 1494–1495
entries.desc: Charles VIII of France invaded Italy to press his claim to Naples, opening decades of Italian Wars among France, Spain, and the Holy Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Appomattox Court House
entries.desc: Confederate general Robert E. Lee surrendered the Army of Northern Virginia to Union forces, effectively ending the American Civil War.

entries.name: 1893 Franco-Siamese crisis
entries.desc: A French gunboat standoff and ultimatum forced Siam to cede territory east of the Mekong, cementing French colonial control of Indochina.

entries.name: Battle of Talikota
entries.desc: An alliance of Deccan sultanates crushed the Vijayanagara Empire, triggering the eventual collapse of the last great Hindu empire of southern India.

entries.name: First Anglo-Maratha War
entries.desc: The British East India Company's first war with the Maratha Empire ended inconclusively after years of fighting across western India.

entries.name: Battle of Köse Dağ
entries.desc: A Mongol army decisively defeated the Seljuk Sultanate of Rum in eastern Anatolia, reducing the sultanate to Mongol vassalage.

entries.name: Battle of Bailén
entries.desc: Spanish forces forced the surrender of a French army corps, the first open-field defeat of a Napoleonic army and a spark for anti-French resistance.

entries.name: Battle of Strasbourg
entries.desc: Roman forces under Julian defeated the Alamanni confederation near modern Strasbourg, securing the Rhine frontier.

entries.name: Battle of Jerusalem
entries.desc: British Empire forces captured Jerusalem from the Ottoman Empire during World War I's Sinai and Palestine campaign.

entries.name: War of the Sixth Coalition
entries.desc: A coalition of European powers defeated Napoleonic France and forced Napoleon's abdication and exile to Elba.

entries.name: Reconquest of Constantinople
entries.desc: Nicaean forces recaptured Constantinople from the Latin Empire, restoring the Byzantine Empire under the Palaiologos dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Velbazhd
entries.desc: Serbian forces defeated the Bulgarian Empire near Velbazhd, shifting the balance of power in the Balkans toward Serbia.

entries.name: Kashmir conflict
entries.desc: A territorial dispute between India and Pakistan over the former princely state of Jammu and Kashmir, ongoing since the 1947 partition of India.

entries.name: Battle of the Tenaru
entries.desc: Allied ground forces repelled a Japanese assault on Guadalcanal, the first major Japanese land offensive of the Pacific campaign.

entries.name: Battle of Mons
entries.desc: The British Expeditionary Force's first major engagement of World War I against the advancing German army in Belgium.

entries.name: Torstenson War
entries.desc: Sweden defeated Denmark–Norway in a conflict tied to the wider Thirty Years' War, boosting Swedish power in the Baltic.

entries.name: Russo-Persian War of 1826–28
entries.desc: Russia's final war with Qajar Iran ended in Persian territorial losses in the South Caucasus under the Treaty of Turkmenchay.

entries.name: Battle of Abritus
entries.desc: A federation of Gothic tribes defeated the Roman army and killed Emperor Decius, the first Roman emperor to die in battle against a foreign enemy.

entries.name: Battle of Cajamarca
entries.desc: Francisco Pizarro's small Spanish force ambushed and captured the Inca ruler Atahualpa, opening the Spanish conquest of the Inca Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Chacabuco
entries.desc: The Army of the Andes under José de San Martín defeated Spanish royalist forces near Santiago, a decisive early victory in the Chilean War of Independence.

entries.name: Battle of the Wilderness
entries.desc: Opening battle of Ulysses S. Grant's 1864 Overland Campaign against Robert E. Lee, fought in dense Virginia woodland during the American Civil War.

entries.name: Battle of Adrianople
entries.desc: Gothic rebels under Fritigern annihilated a Roman army and killed Emperor Valens near Adrianople, a catastrophic defeat for the Eastern Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of Barnet
entries.desc: Decisive Yorkist victory over Lancastrian forces in the Wars of the Roses that helped secure the English throne for Edward IV.

entries.name: War of the League of Cognac
entries.desc: Habsburg forces under Charles V fought an Italian alliance including France, the Papacy, and Venice for control of Italy from 1526 to 1530.

entries.name: Battle of Castle Itter
entries.desc: American, German, and French prisoners jointly defended an Austrian castle against the Waffen-SS in one of the final actions of World War II in Europe.

entries.name: Battle of Maldon
entries.desc: Viking raiders defeated an Anglo-Saxon force led by Earl Byrhtnoth near Maldon in Essex, later commemorated in an Old English poem.

entries.name: Treaty on the Creation of the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics
entries.desc: Declaration and treaty formally establishing the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, centralising several Soviet republics under a Moscow-based federal government.

entries.name: Franco-Turkish War
entries.desc: Also known as the Cilicia Campaign, a conflict between France and Turkish nationalist forces from 1918 to 1921 in the aftermath of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Gumbinnen
entries.desc: A hasty German offensive on the Eastern Front was repelled by the Russian Army near Gumbinnen in East Prussia, early in World War I.

entries.name: Umayyad conquest of Hispania
entries.desc: Umayyad forces conquered the Visigothic Kingdom on the Iberian Peninsula, ending Christian rule across most of Iberia and establishing al-Andalus.

entries.name: Anglo-Afghan Treaty of 1919
entries.desc: Also known as the Treaty of Rawalpindi, this agreement ended the Third Anglo-Afghan War and secured Afghan control over its own foreign affairs.

entries.name: Battle of Klokotnitsa
entries.desc: Bulgarian forces under Ivan Asen II crushed the Empire of Thessalonica near Klokotnitsa, restoring Bulgaria as the dominant power in southeastern Europe.

entries.name: Battle of Teruel
entries.desc: One of the bloodiest engagements of the Spanish Civil War, fought in brutal winter conditions as the city changed hands before falling to Nationalist forces.

entries.name: Battle of the Ice
entries.desc: Alexander Nevsky's Novgorodian forces defeated the Livonian Order on the frozen Lake Peipus, halting German and Danish crusading expansion into Rus' lands.

entries.name: Battle of Belleau Wood
entries.desc: American Marine and Army units, alongside French and British forces, halted the German spring offensive near the Marne River in World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Cape Esperance
entries.desc: A U.S. Navy cruiser force ambushed and defeated a Japanese squadron off Guadalcanal in the Solomon Islands, part of the wider naval campaign there.

entries.name: Battle of Alarcos
entries.desc: Almohad forces under Abu Yusuf Ya'qub al-Mansur crushed the Castilian army of Alfonso VIII, forcing a Christian retreat to Toledo.

entries.name: Battle of Stallupönen
entries.desc: The opening battle of World War I's Eastern Front, in which German forces under Hermann von François defeated a larger but disorganized Russian force.

entries.name: Polish–Lithuanian War
entries.desc: An undeclared conflict between newly independent Lithuania and Poland fought mainly around Vilnius and Suwałki in the aftermath of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Harran
entries.desc: A major Seljuk Turk victory over the Crusader states of Antioch and Edessa near the Mesopotamian city of Harran in 1104.

entries.name: Ecuadorian–Peruvian War
entries.desc: A 1941 border war between Ecuador and Peru, the first of three 20th-century conflicts between the two nations.

entries.name: Portuguese Restoration War
entries.desc: Portugal's uprising against Habsburg Spain beginning in 1640, ending the Iberian Union and restoring Portuguese independence by 1668.

entries.name: Battle of Bapheus
entries.desc: A decisive victory for Osman I's Ottoman forces over the Byzantines in Bithynia, marking the rise of the Ottoman state.

entries.name: Battle of Leipzig
entries.desc: The largest battle of the Napoleonic Wars, in which allied Austrian, Prussian, Russian and Swedish forces decisively defeated Napoleon at Leipzig.

entries.name: Potsdam Conference
entries.desc: A meeting of Allied leaders Truman, Churchill (later Attlee), and Stalin at Potsdam to plan the postwar order after Germany's defeat.

entries.name: Battle of Moscow
entries.desc: A Soviet defensive campaign that halted the German advance on Moscow during the winter of 1941–42.

entries.name: Battle of France
entries.desc: Germany's rapid invasion and defeat of France and the Low Countries in 1940, forcing the Allied evacuation at Dunkirk.

entries.name: Great Northern War
entries.desc: A coalition led by Russia broke Swedish dominance in Northern, Central and Eastern Europe between 1700 and 1721.

entries.name: Revolt of the Comuneros
entries.desc: A Castilian uprising against the rule of Charles I, at its peak controlling the cities of Valladolid, Tordesillas, and Toledo.

entries.name: War of the Pacific
entries.desc: Chile defeated a Bolivian-Peruvian alliance over Atacama Desert resources, gaining territory from both nations.

entries.name: Operation Michael
entries.desc: The opening German offensive of the 1918 spring campaign, launched from the Hindenburg Line to break the Allied lines.

entries.name: Anti-Comintern Pact
entries.desc: An anti-communist alliance between Nazi Germany and Imperial Japan, signed in Berlin and later joined by Italy and other states.

entries.name: Nine Years' War
entries.desc: A European great-power war from 1688 to 1697 pitting France against the Grand Alliance.

entries.name: Battle of Worringen
entries.desc: The decisive battle of the War of the Limburg Succession, fought near Cologne between the Archbishop of Cologne and his rivals.

entries.name: Battle of Poltava
entries.desc: Tsar Peter I's decisive defeat of Charles XII's Swedish army, ending Sweden's status as a European great power.

entries.name: Treaty of friendship and alliance between the Government of Mongolia and Tibet
entries.desc: A 1913 agreement signed at Urga between the governments of Mongolia and Tibet.

entries.name: Battle of Raseiniai
entries.desc: A major tank battle in Lithuania during the opening days of Operation Barbarossa, between German and Soviet armored forces.

entries.name: Locarno Treaties
entries.desc: A set of post-WWI agreements among Germany, France, Britain, Belgium, Italy, Poland and Czechoslovakia guaranteeing western European borders.

entries.name: Tripartite Pact
entries.desc: A defensive military alliance between Germany, Italy, and Japan signed in Berlin, later joined by several other Axis states.

entries.name: Battle of Crete
entries.desc: German airborne invasion of Crete in May 1941, the first major airborne invasion in military history.

entries.name: Second Battle of El Alamein
entries.desc: Allied victory in Egypt that halted the Axis advance in North Africa and marked a turning point in the desert campaign.

entries.name: Austro-Prussian War
entries.desc: Short war in which Prussia defeated Austria, ending Austrian dominance in Germany and paving the way for Prussian-led unification.

entries.name: Battle of Tannenberg
entries.desc: Decisive German victory over the Russian army in East Prussia during the opening weeks of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Angaur
entries.desc: U.S. Army assault on the Japanese-held island of Angaur in Palau during the Pacific War.

entries.name: Battle of Spotsylvania Court House
entries.desc: Bloody two-week clash between Grant's and Lee's armies in Virginia during the American Civil War's Overland Campaign.

entries.name: Hispano-Moroccan War
entries.desc: Spanish military campaign against Morocco fought around Ceuta, resulting in expanded Spanish territory in North Africa.

entries.name: Battle of Berlin
entries.desc: Final major Soviet offensive of World War II in Europe, culminating in the fall of the German capital and Hitler's suicide.

entries.name: Munich agreement
entries.desc: Agreement by Britain, France, Germany, and Italy permitting Nazi annexation of the Sudetenland, widely seen as a failed act of appeasement.

entries.name: Battle of Jena–Auerstedt
entries.desc: Napoleon's twin victories over Prussia that shattered the Prussian army and led to French occupation of Prussia.

entries.name: New Zealand Wars
entries.desc: Series of conflicts between the British Crown and Māori iwi over land and sovereignty in colonial New Zealand.

entries.name: Battle of Blenheim
entries.desc: Decisive Allied victory over French and Bavarian forces during the War of the Spanish Succession, fought in Bavaria.

entries.name: French and Indian War
entries.desc: North American theater of the Seven Years' War, fought between Britain and France, with Native American allies, for colonial control.

entries.name: Lapland War
entries.desc: Conflict in which Finland fought to expel German troops from Finnish Lapland after Finland's armistice with the Soviet Union.

entries.name: Battle of Königgrätz
entries.desc: Decisive Prussian victory over Austria in Bohemia that effectively ended the Austro-Prussian War.

entries.name: Second Schleswig War
entries.desc: War in which Prussia and Austria defeated Denmark, securing the duchies of Schleswig and Holstein for the German states.

entries.name: Battle of Seven Pines
entries.desc: Inconclusive but bloody Civil War battle near Richmond, Virginia, notable for wounding Confederate general Joseph Johnston.

entries.name: Siege of Budapest
entries.desc: Prolonged Soviet siege that captured the Hungarian capital from German and Hungarian forces in the closing months of World War II.

entries.name: Second Battle of Kharkov
entries.desc: German victory that encircled and destroyed several Soviet armies attempting to retake Kharkiv in eastern Ukraine.

entries.name: Battle of Somosierra
entries.desc: Napoleon's forces broke through a Spanish mountain pass defense on the road to Madrid during the Peninsular War.

entries.name: Battle of Narva
entries.desc: Swedish victory over a much larger Russian army at the outset of the Great Northern War in Estonia.

entries.name: Treaty of Rapallo
entries.desc: Agreement between Weimar Germany and Soviet Russia restoring diplomatic relations and renouncing wartime claims.

entries.name: Battle of Sedan
entries.desc: Decisive Prussian victory that captured Napoleon III and effectively ended the Franco-Prussian War.

entries.name: Jay's Treaty
entries.desc: Agreement easing lingering tensions between the United States and Great Britain after the Revolutionary War.

entries.name: War of the Bavarian Succession
entries.desc: Largely bloodless conflict between Austria and Prussia over the succession to the Bavarian throne.

entries.name: Third Battle of Kharkov
entries.desc: German counteroffensive that recaptured the Ukrainian city of Kharkov after the Soviet victory at Stalingrad.

entries.name: Northern Seven Years' War
entries.desc: Baltic conflict between Denmark-Norway and Sweden, ended by the Treaty of Stettin.

entries.name: Battle of Ulm
entries.desc: Napoleon's maneuvers forced the surrender of an Austrian army without a major battle, opening the road to Vienna.

entries.name: Battle of Legnica
entries.desc: Mongol forces defeated a coalition of Polish and allied knights during the Mongol invasion of Europe.

entries.name: Russo-Japanese War
entries.desc: Conflict between the Russian and Japanese empires over Manchuria and Korea, won decisively by Japan.

entries.name: Treaty of Meerssen
entries.desc: Partition of the Carolingian Middle Kingdom between the East and West Frankish rulers.

entries.name: Demyansk Pocket
entries.desc: German forces encircled by the Red Army near Demyansk were resupplied by air for months during WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Ramillies
entries.desc: Marlborough's victory over France secured much of the Spanish Netherlands during the War of the Spanish Succession.

entries.name: First Battle of El Alamein
entries.desc: Allied forces halted the Axis advance into Egypt during the North African campaign of WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Kiev
entries.desc: German encirclement of Soviet forces around Kyiv produced one of the largest encirclements of WWII.

entries.name: Battle of Hürtgen Forest
entries.desc: Prolonged and costly Allied campaign in a forested region of Germany near the Belgian border during WWII.

entries.name: Treaty of Rastatt
entries.desc: Peace agreement between France and Austria that concluded the War of the Spanish Succession.

entries.name: Potsdam Declaration
entries.desc: Allied ultimatum issued from Potsdam demanding Japan's unconditional surrender to end WWII.

entries.name: Ottoman–Safavid War
entries.desc: Conflict between the Ottoman and Safavid empires over control of Iraq and the Caucasus.

entries.name: Battle of Monmouth
entries.desc: Revolutionary War battle in New Jersey where Washington's forces fought the British to a standstill, the war's last major northern engagement.

entries.name: Battle of Mu'tah
entries.desc: Clash between an early Muslim expedition and Byzantine-allied forces near Jordan, a precursor to the Muslim conquest of the Levant.

entries.name: Battle of Guadalajara
entries.desc: Spanish Civil War battle in which Republican forces routed an Italian expeditionary corps sent to aid the Nationalists.

entries.name: Normandy landings
entries.desc: Allied amphibious invasion of northern France that opened the Western Front and hastened Nazi Germany's defeat.

entries.name: Swabian War
entries.desc: Conflict between the Swiss Confederacy and the Habsburgs that secured de facto Swiss independence from the Holy Roman Empire.

entries.name: Battle of the Yser
entries.desc: World War I battle in which Belgian forces flooded the Yser plain to halt the German advance along the coast.

entries.name: Battle of the Bzura
entries.desc: Largest battle of the German invasion of Poland, fought along the Bzura River as Polish forces counterattacked the advancing Wehrmacht.

entries.name: Scanian War
entries.desc: War between Sweden and a coalition led by Denmark-Norway and Brandenburg over control of the Scanian provinces.

entries.name: Battle of Lesnaya
entries.desc: Great Northern War battle in which Russian forces destroyed a Swedish relief column, weakening Charles XII ahead of Poltava.

entries.name: Sack of Amorium
entries.desc: Abbasid Caliphate's destruction of the Byzantine city of Amorium, one of the heaviest defeats the empire suffered.

entries.name: Act of Independence of Lithuania
entries.desc: Declaration proclaiming the restoration of an independent Lithuanian state after more than a century under Russian rule.

entries.name: Operation Greif
entries.desc: German commando operation using English-speaking soldiers in captured American uniforms during the Battle of the Bulge.

entries.name: Balkan Pact
entries.desc: Alliance among Greece, Romania, Turkey, and Yugoslavia intended to preserve the territorial status quo in the Balkans.

entries.name: Boer Wars
entries.desc: Conflicts between the British Empire and the Boer republics over control of South Africa and its goldfields.

entries.name: Baltic Entente
entries.desc: Alliance among Estonia, Latvia, and Lithuania for mutual political cooperation between the two world wars.

entries.name: Schmalkaldic War
entries.desc: War between Holy Roman Emperor Charles V and the Protestant Schmalkaldic League over religious and political control of Germany.

entries.name: Battle of Talavera
entries.desc: Peninsular War battle in which Anglo-Spanish forces under Wellington fought Napoleon's army to a costly standstill in Spain.

entries.name: Second Sino-Japanese War
entries.desc: Major war between the Republic of China and the Empire of Japan that began in 1937 and later merged into the Pacific theatre of World War II.

entries.name: Armistice of 22 June 1940
entries.desc: Agreement signed at Compiègne ending the Battle of France, dividing the country into a German-occupied zone and unoccupied Vichy France.

entries.name: Battle of Kunersdorf
entries.desc: Decisive Austro-Russian victory over Prussia's Frederick the Great during the Seven Years' War, among his worst defeats.

entries.name: Battle of Tours
entries.desc: Frankish and Burgundian forces under Charles Martel halted an Umayyad invasion force in western France.

entries.name: Battle of the Bismarck Sea
entries.desc: Allied aircraft destroyed a Japanese troop convoy bound for New Guinea, crippling Japan's ability to reinforce the Pacific front.

entries.name: Battle of Dresden
entries.desc: Napoleon's last major victory on German soil, fought against the Sixth Coalition.

entries.name: Battle of Tinian
entries.desc: US forces captured the Mariana Islands island of Tinian from Japan; it later served as the launch site for the atomic bomb missions.

entries.name: Slovak–Hungarian War
entries.desc: Brief border conflict between the newly independent Slovak state and Hungary over territory in eastern Slovakia.

entries.name: Altmark Incident
entries.desc: A British destroyer boarded the German supply ship Altmark in Norwegian waters to free Allied prisoners of war.

entries.name: First Balkan War
entries.desc: Balkan League states defeated the Ottoman Empire, stripping it of nearly all its remaining European territory.

entries.name: Battle of Mohács
entries.desc: Ottoman forces crushed the Kingdom of Hungary, killing its king and opening the way for Ottoman expansion into central Europe.

entries.name: Treaty of Devol
entries.desc: Agreement between Bohemond I of Antioch and Byzantine Emperor Alexios I, subordinating the Principality of Antioch to Byzantium.

entries.name: First Sino-Japanese War
entries.desc: War between Qing China and Meiji Japan over influence in Korea, ending in a decisive Japanese victory.

entries.name: Battle of Shiroyama
entries.desc: Final battle of the Satsuma Rebellion in Japan, in which imperial forces crushed Saigō Takamori's samurai rebels.

entries.name: Pontiac's War
entries.desc: A confederation of Native American tribes fought British forces across the Great Lakes region after the French and Indian War.

entries.name: French invasion of Russia
entries.desc: Napoleon's catastrophic campaign into Russia devastated the Grande Armée and marked a turning point in his downfall.

entries.name: Battle of Río Salado
entries.desc: Christian Castilian and Portuguese forces defeated a Marinid-Nasrid invasion, ending major Muslim offensives on the Iberian Peninsula.

entries.name: Battle of Kosovo
entries.desc: Clash between Serbian and Ottoman forces in Kosovo that became a foundational event in Serbian national memory.

entries.name: Battle of Iwo Jima
entries.desc: Brutal US Marine assault to capture the island from Japan, immortalized by the flag-raising on Mount Suribachi.

entries.name: Treaty of Tordesillas
entries.desc: Agreement between Spain and Portugal dividing newly discovered lands outside Europe along a meridian in the Atlantic.

entries.name: Egyptian–Wahhabi War
entries.desc: Ottoman-Egyptian campaign that crushed the First Saudi State and ended Wahhabi control of the Hejaz.

entries.name: Paris Convention for the Protection of Industrial Property
entries.desc: International treaty signed in Paris establishing the first global framework for protecting patents, trademarks, and industrial designs.

entries.name: Second Balkan War
entries.desc: Bulgaria attacked its former allies over Macedonia and was defeated, losing most of its First Balkan War gains.

entries.name: Battle of Borodino
entries.desc: Bloodiest single day of the Napoleonic Wars, fought near Moscow between Napoleon's Grande Armée and the Russian army.

entries.name: Ten Years' War
entries.desc: First major Cuban uprising against Spanish colonial rule, a precursor to the later Cuban War of Independence.

entries.name: Dakota War of 1862
entries.desc: Armed conflict between the Dakota Sioux and the United States in Minnesota, ending in the mass execution of 38 Dakota men.

entries.name: Georgian–Armenian War
entries.desc: Brief border war between the newly independent republics of Georgia and Armenia over disputed territories.

entries.name: Polish–Soviet War
entries.desc: Conflict over the borders of the newly restored Polish state, ending with Poland halting the Soviet advance at Warsaw.

entries.name: Dutch Revolt
entries.desc: Revolt of the Low Countries against Spanish Habsburg rule, leading to the independence of the Dutch Republic.

entries.name: War of the First Coalition
entries.desc: First coalition of European monarchies to fight revolutionary France, ending in French territorial gains.

entries.name: Battle of Agincourt
entries.desc: Decisive English victory over a much larger French force during the Hundred Years' War.

entries.name: Bretton Woods system
entries.desc: Post-war international monetary order agreed at a conference in New Hampshire, pegging currencies to the US dollar and gold.

entries.name: Battle of Halidon Hill
entries.desc: English victory over Scottish forces during the Second War of Scottish Independence.

entries.name: Battles of Khalkhin Gol
entries.desc: Soviet-Mongolian victory over Japanese forces on the Mongolia-Manchuria border, ending Japanese expansion northward.

entries.name: First Battle of the Marne
entries.desc: Allied counteroffensive that halted the German advance into France early in the First World War.

entries.name: First Opium War
entries.desc: Conflict between Britain and Qing China over the opium trade, ending with China ceding Hong Kong to Britain.

entries.name: Battle of Okinawa
entries.desc: Largest amphibious assault of the Pacific War, resulting in massive casualties as Allied forces took the island of Okinawa.

entries.name: Operation Pedestal
entries.desc: British convoy operation that fought through heavy Axis air and naval attacks to resupply besieged Malta.

entries.name: Battle of Eniwetok
entries.desc: US forces captured Eniwetok Atoll from Japanese defenders, securing a key base in the Marshall Islands.

entries.name: Battle of Yarmouk
entries.desc: Decisive Rashidun Caliphate victory over the Byzantine Empire that opened the Levant to Muslim conquest.

entries.name: General Agreement on Tariffs and Trade
entries.desc: Multilateral treaty signed in Geneva to reduce tariffs and promote international trade, later evolving into the WTO.

entries.name: Anglo-Nepalese War
entries.desc: Conflict between the British East India Company and the Gurkha Kingdom of Nepal that redrew the Himalayan frontier.

entries.name: Ingrian War
entries.desc: Swedish-Russian conflict over control of Ingria and northwestern Russia during Russia's Time of Troubles.

entries.name: Livonian Crusade
entries.desc: Baltic campaigns by German and Danish crusaders to conquer and Christianize the pagan peoples of Livonia and Estonia.

entries.name: Battle of Dyrrhachium
entries.desc: Norman victory over the Byzantine Empire on the Adriatic coast that opened Robert Guiscard's campaign into the Balkans.

entries.name: Akkerman Convention
entries.desc: Treaty between the Russian and Ottoman Empires settling disputes over Serbia and the Danubian Principalities.

entries.name: Battle of Megiddo
entries.desc: Decisive British-led offensive in Palestine that shattered Ottoman forces and hastened the empire's collapse.

entries.name: Battle of Manzikert
entries.desc: Seljuk victory over the Byzantine Empire that opened Anatolia to Turkish settlement.

entries.name: Battle of Jaffa
entries.desc: Richard the Lionheart's defense of Jaffa against Saladin during the Third Crusade.

entries.name: Battle of Vienna
entries.desc: Coalition victory that broke the Ottoman siege of Vienna and halted Ottoman expansion into Central Europe.

entries.name: Armenian–Azerbaijani War
entries.desc: Territorial conflict between the newly independent Armenian and Azerbaijani republics after the Russian Empire's collapse.

entries.name: Battle of Poitiers
entries.desc: Major English victory during the Hundred Years' War in which King John II of France was captured.

entries.name: Night attack at Târgoviște
entries.desc: Surprise nighttime assault by Vlad the Impaler on the Ottoman army encamped in Wallachia.

entries.name: Operation Faustschlag
entries.desc: German offensive against Soviet Russia that forced the Bolsheviks to accept the Treaty of Brest-Litovsk.

entries.name: Battle of Chains
entries.desc: Early Rashidun victory under Khalid ibn al-Walid over Sasanian forces in Mesopotamia.

entries.name: Battle of Stamford Bridge
entries.desc: King Harold II's victory over Norwegian invaders in England, ending Viking claims to the English throne.

entries.name: Battle of Plassey
entries.desc: British East India Company victory over the Nawab of Bengal that established British colonial control in India.

entries.name: Battle of Hannut
entries.desc: A major tank battle in Belgium during the German invasion of the Low Countries, one of the largest armored clashes of the early war.

entries.name: Italo-Turkish War
entries.desc: Italy seized the Ottoman provinces of Tripolitania and Cyrenaica, forming the colony of Libya and marking the first military use of aircraft.

entries.name: Battle of Wuhan
entries.desc: One of the largest battles of the Second Sino-Japanese War, fought over the strategic tri-city area of Wuhan in central China.

entries.name: Battle of Tampere
entries.desc: A decisive battle of the Finnish Civil War in which White forces captured the Red stronghold of Tampere.

entries.name: Kellogg-Briand pact
entries.desc: An international agreement, signed in Paris, in which signatory states renounced war as an instrument of national policy.

entries.name: Battle of Breitenfeld
entries.desc: A decisive Swedish and Saxon victory over Catholic League forces during the Thirty Years' War, marking Sweden's emergence as a major power.

entries.name: Battle of the Little Bighorn
entries.desc: Lakota, Cheyenne, and Arapaho forces led by Crazy Horse and Sitting Bull annihilated Custer's 7th Cavalry detachment in Montana.

entries.name: Battle of the Bridge
entries.desc: A rare early defeat for the Rashidun Caliphate's forces, routed by a Sasanian army after crossing the Euphrates on a pontoon bridge.

entries.name: French Revolutionary Wars
entries.desc: A series of conflicts between Revolutionary France and a coalition of European monarchies seeking to contain and reverse the Revolution.

entries.name: Siege of Fort William Henry
entries.desc: French and Native American forces besieged and captured a British fort in colonial New York during the French and Indian War.

entries.name: Lazic War
entries.desc: A protracted conflict between the Byzantine and Sasanian empires for control of Lazica, on the eastern Black Sea coast.

entries.name: Battle of Dunkirk
entries.desc: German forces trapped Allied troops on the French coast, prompting a large-scale evacuation across the English Channel.

entries.name: Battle of Mactan
entries.desc: Filipino chieftain Lapu-Lapu's warriors repelled a Spanish expedition and killed explorer Ferdinand Magellan on Mactan Island.

entries.name: Mongol invasions and conquests
entries.desc: Beginning under Genghis Khan in Mongolia, Mongol armies built the largest contiguous land empire in history, stretching across Asia and into Europe.

entries.name: Eighth Russo-Turkish War
entries.desc: A war between the Russian and Ottoman empires over Balkan and Black Sea territories, ending with the Treaty of Bucharest.

entries.name: Battle of Ankara
entries.desc: Timur's forces decisively defeated and captured the Ottoman sultan Bayezid I, plunging the Ottoman Empire into a period of civil war.

entries.name: Battle of Kulikovo
entries.desc: A coalition of Russian principalities led by Dmitri Donskoi defeated the Golden Horde, a turning point in Russian resistance to Mongol rule.

entries.name: Montevideo Convention
entries.desc: A treaty signed in Montevideo, Uruguay, codifying the widely cited criteria for statehood in international law.

entries.name: Sykes–Picot Agreement
entries.desc: A secret Anglo-French agreement that divided the Ottoman Empire's Arab provinces into spheres of influence, shaping the modern Middle East.

entries.name: Battle of Nicopolis
entries.desc: An Ottoman army under Bayezid I crushed a large European crusading force near the Danube, ending hopes of relieving Christian rule in the Balkans.

entries.name: War of the Polish Succession
entries.desc: A European conflict over the Polish throne, fought mainly in Italy and the Rhineland, pitting France and Spain against Austria and Russia.

entries.name: Philippine–American War
entries.desc: An armed conflict between the First Philippine Republic and the United States following the Spanish-American War, resulting in American colonial rule of the islands.

entries.name: Turkish Abductions
entries.desc: Barbary corsairs from North Africa raided Iceland, killing dozens and carrying several hundred captives into slavery in Algiers.

entries.name: Second Boer War
entries.desc: A war between the British Empire and the Boer republics of the Transvaal and Orange Free State for control of southern Africa's goldfields.

entries.name: Union of Utrecht
entries.desc: An alliance of northern Dutch provinces against Spanish rule that laid the constitutional foundation for the Dutch Republic.

entries.name: Berne Convention for the Protection of Literary and Artistic Works
entries.desc: An international agreement establishing mutual recognition of copyright between signatory nations, negotiated in Berne, Switzerland.

entries.name: Marco Polo Bridge incident
entries.desc: A clash between Chinese and Japanese troops near Beijing that escalated into full-scale war between China and Japan.

entries.name: Battle of Las Navas de Tolosa
entries.desc: A coalition of Christian Iberian kingdoms decisively defeated the Almohad Caliphate, a turning point in the Reconquista.

entries.name: Soviet–Japanese War
entries.desc: The Soviet Union's invasion of Japanese-held Manchuria, Korea, and the Kuril Islands in the final days of World War II.

entries.name: First Battle of Bull Run
entries.desc: The first major land battle of the American Civil War, ending in a Confederate victory near Manassas, Virginia.

entries.name: Treaty of the Pyrenees
entries.desc: A peace treaty ending the Franco-Spanish War and establishing the modern border between France and Spain along the Pyrenees.

entries.name: Battle of the Ardennes
entries.desc: An early clash of World War I in which French and German armies fought inconclusively in the forested Ardennes region.

entries.name: War of the Quadruple Alliance
entries.desc: Britain, France, Austria, and the Dutch Republic united to check Spanish expansion in Italy under Cardinal Alberoni's foreign policy.

entries.name: Brusilov Offensive
entries.desc: A massive Russian offensive against Austro-Hungarian forces on the Eastern Front, one of the most successful Allied operations of World War I.

entries.name: Battle of Bosworth Field
entries.desc: Henry Tudor defeated and killed Richard III, ending the Wars of the Roses and founding the Tudor dynasty.

entries.name: Battle of Bannockburn
entries.desc: Robert the Bruce's Scottish forces decisively defeated the English army of Edward II, securing Scottish independence.

entries.name: Chaco War
entries.desc: A brutal war between Bolivia and Paraguay over the arid Gran Chaco region, the deadliest interstate conflict in 20th-century South America.

entries.name: Battle of Rymnik
entries.desc: A decisive Russo-Austrian victory over Ottoman forces during the Russo-Turkish War, led by Alexander Suvorov.

entries.name: Greco-Italian War
entries.desc: Fascist Italy's invasion of Greece, which was repelled by Greek forces and drew Nazi Germany into the Balkan campaign.

entries.name: Battle of Prague
entries.desc: A Prussian victory over Austrian forces early in the Seven Years' War, followed by a costly Prussian siege of the Bohemian capital.

entries.name: Battle of Rovine
entries.desc: Wallachian prince Mircea the Elder repelled an Ottoman invasion force, delaying the Ottoman conquest of Wallachia.

entries.name: Battle of Brunanburh
entries.desc: King Æthelstan's army crushed a coalition of Scots, Vikings, and Strathclyde Britons, a defining moment in English unification.

entries.name: Anglo-Dutch Treaty of 1824
entries.desc: Britain and the Netherlands divided their colonial spheres of influence in the Malay Archipelago along the Strait of Malacca.

entries.name: Treaty of Batum
entries.desc: The Ottoman Empire imposed a peace settlement on the newly independent South Caucasus republics of Armenia, Georgia, and Azerbaijan.

entries.name: Anglo-Zulu War
entries.desc: Britain invaded the Zulu Kingdom in southern Africa, suffering a stunning early defeat at Isandlwana before conquering Zululand.

entries.name: Battle of Wadi al-Khaznadar
entries.desc: Mongol Ilkhanate forces under Ghazan Khan defeated the Mamluk Sultanate near Homs, Syria.

entries.name: Battle of the Gates of Trajan
entries.desc: Bulgarian forces under Tsar Samuel ambushed and destroyed a Byzantine army in the mountains of eastern Bulgaria.

entries.name: Treaty of Craiova
entries.desc: Romania ceded Southern Dobruja to Bulgaria under pressure from Nazi Germany and the Soviet Union.

entries.name: Battle of Hunayn
entries.desc: Muhammad's Muslim army defeated the Hawazin and Thaqif tribes near Mecca, securing control of the Arabian Peninsula's holy cities.

entries.name: Battle of Edington
entries.desc: King Alfred the Great of Wessex decisively defeated the Great Heathen Army of Danish Vikings, securing Anglo-Saxon England's survival.

entries.name: Morean War
entries.desc: Venice and its Holy League allies fought the Ottoman Empire for control of the Peloponnese peninsula in Greece.

entries.name: Battle of Karánsebes
entries.desc: Austrian army units mistakenly attacked each other in a chaotic nighttime panic during the Austro-Turkish War, inflicting heavy self-caused casualties.

entries.name: Williamite War in Ireland
entries.desc: Supporters of deposed King James II fought forces loyal to William of Orange for control of Ireland and the English throne.

entries.name: Iberian War
entries.desc: The Byzantine and Sasanian Persian empires fought for control of the Caucasian kingdom of Iberia, in modern-day Georgia.

entries.name: Byzantine–Sassanid War of 602–628
entries.desc: The last and most devastating war between the Byzantine and Sasanian Persian empires ended in a decisive Byzantine victory that exhausted both sides before the Arab conquests.

entries.name: Mongol invasion of Central Asia
entries.desc: Genghis Khan's Mongol armies destroyed the Khwarezmian Empire, conquering Transoxiana and Persia's eastern territories.

entries.name: Second Battle of Ypres
entries.desc: German forces used chlorine gas for the first time on the Western Front in an assault on Allied lines near Ypres, Belgium.

entries.name: Second Battle of Bull Run
entries.desc: Confederate forces under Robert E. Lee and Stonewall Jackson decisively defeated the Union Army of Virginia near Manassas, Virginia.

entries.name: Second Battle of St Albans
entries.desc: The Second Battle of St Albans was fought on 17 February 1461 during the Wars of the Roses in St Albans, Hertfordshire, England.

entries.name: Second Anglo-Burmese War
entries.desc: The Second Anglo-Burmese War or the Second Burma War was the second of the three wars fought between the Burmese Empire and British Empire during the 19th century. The war resulted in a British victory with more Burmese territory being annexed to British India.

entries.name: Second Barbary War
entries.desc: The Second Barbary War, also known as the U.S.–Algerian War and the Algerine War, was a brief military conflict between the United States and the North African state of Algiers in 1815.

entries.name: Second Anglo-Afghan War
entries.desc: The Second Anglo-Afghan War was a military conflict fought between the British Raj and the Emirate of Afghanistan from 1878 to 1880, when the latter was ruled by Sher Ali Khan of the Barakzai dynasty, the son of former Emir Dost Mohammad Khan. The war was part of the Great Game between the British and Russian empires.

entries.name: Second Hundred Years' War
entries.desc: The Second Hundred Years' War is a term of periodization, or a historical era designation, coined by J. R. Seeley in his work The Expansion of England (1883).

entries.name: Battle of Hattin
entries.desc: The Battle of Hattin took place on 4 July 1187, between the Crusader states of the Levant and the forces of the Ayyubid sultan Saladin. It is also known as the Battle of the Horns of Hattin, due to the shape of the nearby extinct volcano of that name.

entries.name: Second Anglo-Maratha War
entries.desc: Second Anglo-Maratha War was a large conflict within the Maratha Empire involving the East India Company. It resulted in a major loss of territory for the Marathas, including regions around Delhi and in present-day Gujarat falling into direct Company rule.

entries.name: Second Anglo-Mysore War
entries.desc: The Second Anglo-Mysore War was a conflict between the Kingdom of Mysore and the British East India Company from 1780 to 1784. At the time, Mysore was a key French ally in India, and the conflict between Britain against the French and Dutch in the American Revolutionary War influenced Anglo-Mysorean hostilities in India. The great majority of soldiers on the company side were raised, trained, p…

entries.name: Second Silesian War
entries.desc: The Second Silesian War was a war between Prussia and Austria that lasted from 1744 to 1745 and confirmed Prussia's control of the region of Silesia. The war was fought mainly in Silesia, Bohemia, and Upper Saxony and formed one theatre of the wider War of the Austrian Succession. It was the second of three Silesian Wars fought between Frederick the Great's Prussia and Maria Theresa's Austria i…

entries.name: First Battle of the Aisne
entries.desc: The First Battle of the Aisne was the Allied follow-up offensive against the right wing of the German First Army and the Second Army as they retreated after the First Battle of the Marne earlier in September 1914. The Advance to the Aisne consisted of the Battle of the Marne (7–10 September) and the Battle of the Aisne (12–15 September).

entries.name: Battle of Sekigahara
entries.desc: The Battle of Sekigahara was an important battle in Japan which occurred on October 21, 1600 in what is now Gifu Prefecture, Japan, at the end of the Sengoku period.

entries.name: Expulsion of the Moriscos
entries.desc: The Expulsion of the Moriscos was decreed by King Philip III of Spain on April 9, 1609. The Moriscos were descendants of Spain's Muslim population who had been forced to convert to Christianity. Since the Spanish were fighting wars in the Americas, feeling threatened by the Ottomans raiding along the Spanish coast and by two Morisco revolts in the century since Islam was outlawed in Spain, it s…

entries.name: Battle of Kumanovo
entries.desc: The Battle of Kumanovo, on 23–24 October 1912, was a major battle of the First Balkan War. It was an important Serbian victory over the Ottoman army in the Kosovo Vilayet, shortly after the outbreak of the war. After this defeat, the Ottoman army abandoned the major part of the region, suffering heavy losses in manpower and in war materiel.

entries.name: Battle of the Alamo
entries.desc: The Battle of the Alamo was a pivotal event and military engagement in the Texas Revolution. Following a 13-day siege, Mexican troops under President General Antonio López de Santa Anna reclaimed the Alamo Mission near San Antonio de Béxar.

entries.name: Malayan Emergency
entries.desc: The Malayan Emergency, also known as the Anti–British National Liberation War, (1948–1960) was a guerrilla war fought in Malaya between communist pro-independence fighters of the Malayan National Liberation Army (MNLA) and the military forces of the Federation of Malaya and the Commonwealth. The communists fought to win independence for Malaya from the British Empire and to establish a communis…

entries.name: Battle of Buxar
entries.desc: The Battle of Buxar was fought between 22 and 23 October 1764, between the forces of the British East India Company, under the command of Major Hector Munro, against the combined armies of Shah Alam II, the Emperor of the Mughal Empire; Mir Qasim, the Nawab of Bengal; Balwant Singh, the Maharaja of the Benares State; Shuja-ud-daula, the Nawab of Awadh.

entries.name: Unification Act
entries.desc: The Unification Act was an agreement signed by the Ukrainian People's Republic and the West Ukrainian People's Republic in Sophia Square in Kyiv on 22 January 1919. Since 1999, it is celebrated every year as the Day of Unity of Ukraine to commemorate the signing of the treaty; it is a state holiday in Ukraine, though not a public holiday.

entries.name: 1948 Ashgabat earthquake
entries.desc: The 1948 Ashgabat earthquake was on 6 October with a surface-wave magnitude of 7.3 and a maximum Mercalli intensity of X (Extreme), in Turkmenistan near Ashgabat. Due to censorship by the Soviet government, the event was not widely reported in the Soviet media. Historians tend to agree that the ban on reporting the extent of the casualties and damage did not allow the Soviet government to alloc…

entries.name: Battle of Arras
entries.desc:
The name Battle of Arras refers to a number of battles which took place near the town of Arras in Artois, France:Siege of Arras (1640), a siege by the French against the Spanish during the Thirty Years' War
Battle of Arras (1654), a clash between the French and the Spanish
Battle of Arras (1914), a battle during the Race to the Sea in the First World War
Battle of Arras (1915), took place on Ma…

entries.name: Battle of Friedland
entries.desc: The Battle of Friedland was a major engagement of the Napoleonic Wars between the armies of the French Empire and the Russian Empire led by Napoleon I and General Levin August von Bennigsen respectively. Napoleon and the French obtained a decisive victory that routed much of the Russian army, which retreated chaotically over the Alle river by the end of the fighting. The battlefield is located …

entries.name: Ukrainian–Soviet War
entries.desc: A war fought between 1917 and 1921 between the Ukrainian People's Republic and the Bolsheviks following the Russian Revolution.

entries.name: Battle of Caporetto
entries.desc: A major World War I battle on the Isonzo front, where German and Austro-Hungarian forces routed the Italian army.

entries.name: Second Anglo-Sikh war
entries.desc: A war between the Sikh Empire and the East India Company that ended in the fall of the Sikh Empire and annexation of the Punjab.

entries.name: Second Vienna Award
entries.desc: A territorial arbitration by Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy that transferred Northern Transylvania from Romania to Hungary.

entries.name: Franco-Russian Alliance
entries.desc: A military alliance between France and Russia formed 1891–94 in response to the Triple Alliance, lasting until 1917.

entries.name: Battle of Liaoyang
entries.desc: A major land battle of the Russo-Japanese War fought near Liaoyang in present-day Liaoning Province, China.

entries.name: Battle of Ain Jalut
entries.desc: A battle between the Mamluks of Egypt and the Mongol Ilkhanate near Ain Jalut, marking the first major halt to Mongol expansion.

entries.name: Siege of Belgrade
entries.desc: The 1456 siege in which Hungarian forces under John Hunyadi repelled an Ottoman attempt to capture Belgrade.

entries.name: Battle of Yamama
entries.desc: A decisive battle of the Ridda Wars in which forces of Caliph Abu Bakr defeated the self-proclaimed prophet Musaylima.

entries.name: Livonian War
entries.desc: A 1558–1583 war for control of Old Livonia between the Tsardom of Russia and a coalition of Nordic and Polish-Lithuanian powers.

entries.name: Battle of Nahrawan
entries.desc: A 658 CE battle in which Caliph Ali's forces defeated the Kharijites, former allies who had broken with him after Siffin.

entries.name: Muslim conquest of the Levant
entries.desc: The Rashidun Caliphate's conquest of Byzantine Syria between 634–638 CE, part of the wider early Muslim conquests.

entries.name: War of the Third Coalition
entries.desc: The first conflict of the Napoleonic Wars, pitting Napoleon's France against a coalition led by Britain, Austria, and Russia.

entries.name: Battle of Tarawa
entries.desc: A bloody 1943 amphibious battle between the US and Japan on Tarawa Atoll in the Gilbert Islands, part of Operation Galvanic.

entries.name: Battle of Sarikamish
entries.desc: A disastrous World War I engagement between Russian and Ottoman forces in the Caucasus campaign, fought in winter 1914–15.

entries.name: Hungarian–Romanian War
entries.desc: A 1918–1919 war between Hungary and Romania fought amid the collapse of Austria-Hungary after World War I.

entries.name: Italian War of 1551–1559
entries.desc: A war begun by Henry II of France against Charles V for control of Italy, ended by the Treaty of Cateau-Cambrésis.

entries.name: Crimean War
entries.desc: A war fought 1853–56 between Russia and an alliance of the Ottoman Empire, France, Britain, and Sardinia over the Eastern Question.

entries.name: Battle of Shanghai
entries.desc: A major 1937 battle of the Second Sino-Japanese War fought in Shanghai, among the largest battles of the entire war.

entries.name: Great stand on the Ugra river
entries.desc: An 1480 standoff on the Ugra River between Akhmat Khan of the Great Horde and Grand Prince Ivan III of Moscow, ending Mongol rule over Russia.

entries.name: Battle of Baku
entries.desc: A 1918 battle for control of Baku fought between Ottoman-Azerbaijani forces and Bolshevik-Armenian Baku Commune defenders, later reinforced by British troops.

entries.name: Anastasian War
entries.desc: A war fought from 502 to 506 between the Byzantine and Sasanian empires, the first major conflict between the two powers in over sixty years.

entries.name: Wars of the Three Kingdoms
entries.desc: A series of conflicts from 1639 to 1653 across England, Scotland and Ireland, including the English Civil Wars and the Irish Confederate Wars.

entries.name: First Egyptian-Ottoman War
entries.desc: An 1831–1833 conflict in which Egyptian forces under Muhammad Ali Pasha seized Ottoman Syria, demanding it as reward for aiding the sultan in the Greek War of Independence.

entries.name: War of Devolution
entries.desc: A 1667–1668 war in which France occupied the Spanish Netherlands and Franche-Comté, with Louis XIV claiming the territories through his wife's inheritance rights.

entries.name: Treaty of Gandamak
entries.desc: An 1879 treaty ending the first phase of the Second Anglo-Afghan War, in which Afghan emir Mohammad Yaqub Khan ceded frontier territory and control of foreign affairs to Britain.

entries.name: Second Egyptian-Ottoman War
entries.desc: An 1839–1841 conflict, also called the Second Syrian War, fought mainly in Syria between Egyptian and Ottoman forces.

entries.name: War of 1812
entries.desc: A conflict from 1812 to 1815 between the United States and Britain, fought mainly in North America and at sea amid the wider Napoleonic Wars.

entries.name: Capture of Rome
entries.desc: The 1870 seizure of Rome and the Papal States by forces of the Kingdom of Italy, completing the unification of Italy.

entries.name: Battles of Rzhev
entries.desc: A series of costly Red Army offensives against the Wehrmacht around Rzhev from January 1942 to March 1943 on the Eastern Front of World War II.

entries.name: Battle of Konotop
entries.desc: A 1659 battle in which a Cossack coalition under Hetman Ivan Vyhovsky defeated Russian Tsardom cavalry near Konotop, Ukraine, during the Russo-Polish War.

entries.name: Japanese coup d'état in French Indochina
entries.desc: A March 1945 Japanese operation that overthrew French colonial rule in Indochina as Japan faced imminent Allied invasion late in World War II.

entries.name: Battle of Tolbiac
entries.desc: A battle between the Franks under Clovis I and the Alamanni, traditionally dated to 496, near what is now Zülpich, Germany.

entries.name: German invasion of the Netherlands
entries.desc: A May 1940 German military campaign that overran the Netherlands in days, part of the wider invasion of the Low Countries and France.

entries.name: Battle of Dandanaqan
entries.desc: An 1040 battle in which the Seljuq Turks decisively defeated the Ghaznavid Empire near Merv, ending Ghaznavid dominance over Greater Khorasan.

entries.name: Northwest Indian War
entries.desc: A conflict for control of the Northwest Territory between the United States and a confederation of Native American nations, considered the first American Indian War.

entries.name: Prussian Crusade
entries.desc: A 13th-century series of campaigns by the Teutonic Knights and other Catholic crusaders to conquer and Christianize the pagan Old Prussians.

entries.name: War of the Second Coalition
entries.desc: An 1798–1802 war between revolutionary France and a coalition of European monarchies led by Britain, Austria and Russia.
